commentary_text: The sportsbooks assign Algeria a 62% win probability versus the model's 45%, a 17-percentage-point gap favoring the market's conviction in an Algeria victory. This sharpening could reflect recent form, lineup changes, or injury status that the model cannot access; sportsbooks incorporate real-time information flows that statistical models trained on historical Elo data do not capture. Polymarket sentiment sits between the two sources, offering less extreme confidence in any outcome. The dashboard flags this as a signal worth monitoring—the market's heightened Algeria conviction may rest on current information, but the gap itself remains unresolved without direct access to team news.
